evidence_quote: The University of Houston – Downtown (UHD) all-hazards Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is the underlying framework for Continuity of Operations, ensuring operations are performed efficiently with minimal disruption during an emergency.
catalog_notes: Full all-hazards COOP signed by the President. Four-phase concept of operations (Readiness/Preparedness, Activation, Operations, Reconstitution). Marked For Official Use Only but published publicly; contains all FCD elements including orders of succession, delegations of authority, essential records, alternate/continuity facilities, communications, devolution, reconstitution, and training/testing/exercising. Defines 12-hour relocation and 30-day operating period.

institution_name: University of Texas Health Science Center at Houston (UTHealth Houston)
evidence_quote: The UTHealth Houston Business Continuity Plan (BCP) is intended to describe actions and decision-making capabilities for that time frame by suggesting enhanced pre-event strategies to reduce or eliminate the impacts of emergencies at the local operational level.
catalog_notes: Academic health science center BCP for infrastructure/support services, a companion to the Emergency Management Plan. References a separate IT Disaster Recovery Plan covering ~30 critical systems (PeopleSoft, Epic EHR, Canvas) with RTO under 48 hours and RPO from minutes to hours. Organized around essential functions, facility availability, and 30-day post-event recovery window based on Tropical Storm Allison experience.

evidence_quote: Business continuity planning is broken into four phases: 1. Readiness and Preparedness 2. Activation and Relocation 3. Continuity of Operations 4. Reconstitution of Operations. These four phases encompass the entire business continuity cycle, through planning, activation, and reconstitution.
catalog_notes: UT Austin Office of Emergency Management business continuity program organized around a four-phase continuity life cycle. The university COOP is the institutional framework; colleges/schools/units develop Business Continuity Plans (BCPs). UT Austin also hosts the Higher Education Business Continuity Network (HEBCoN-US).

framework_alignment: Texas State Office of Risk Management Continuity Planning / Texas Essential Functions; FEMA EMI Continuity Practitioner
evidence_quote: Continuity of operations planning is an ongoing process that includes the following elements: identification of essential functions, delegations of authority, orders of succession, communication, vital records and data, continuity facilities, reconstitution, testing, training and exercise, risk assessment, recovery strategies, and a disaster recovery plan.
catalog_notes: University policy UPPS 04.05.22 establishing the campus-wide COOP program, required by the Texas State Office of Risk Management and Texas Labor Code Section 412.054. EHSREM maintains the Texas State COOP and a COOP template used by divisions/colleges via the Texas State Ready (Kuali-style) database. Lists all standard FCD continuity elements.

published_revised: Quick Start Guide dated March 2023; page modified 2026-05-29
evidence_quote: A continuity plan is a collection of resources, actions, procedures, and information that is developed, exercised, and kept ready in the event of a disruption of operations. It helps departments continue their essential functions after a disaster or major disruption.
catalog_notes: Texas A&M Emergency Management Continuity Planning program. Institution publishes a Continuity Planning Quick Start Guide, an Essential Functions Worksheet, and a departmental Continuity Plan Template (Word doc) for units to complete; coordinated by the Business Continuity Program Coordinator within the Office of Safety and Security. Departments submit completed plans into Laserfiche.

evidence_quote: The University of North Texas utilizes Kuali Ready, an online program, to create and manage all Continuity Plans.
catalog_notes: UNT Risk Management Services / Emergency Management & Safety Services continuity program. Uses Kuali Ready to manage all continuity plans; publishes continuity checklists for staff, faculty, students, and researchers (2026) plus a Business Impact Analysis questionnaire and Business Process Analysis questionnaire templates.

evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations Planning Program ensures that the organization is capable of conducting its essential mission and functions under all threats and conditions.
catalog_notes: University of Houston Office of Emergency Management COOP program, transitioning from fillable templates to the Veoci system. Publishes COOP liaison training, emergency relocation position info sheets, essential/non-essential personnel listing, research continuity checklist, and links to a Canvas-based instructional/academic continuity program.

institution_name: University of Texas at San Antonio
evidence_quote: Through careful planning and preparation, the Business Continuity & Emergency Management (BCEM) team helps ensure the university effectively maintains essential functions and research activities at all times.
catalog_notes: UTSA Business Continuity & Emergency Management program. BCEM provides a departmental survey to collect essential-functions information and develop recovery processes; the university uses the Veoci platform to build and manage business continuity plans.

published_revised: Sample completed by Risk Management (testing dated 2020)
evidence_quote: To be better prepared, South Texas College (College) departments should use this template to complete a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP). This plan will describe how these departments will operate essential functions following an emergency or business interruption.
catalog_notes: Departmental COOP template published by South Texas College Risk Management (Environmental Health & Safety) for its own units, with a worked example for the Accountability, Risk & Compliance department. Lists institution-wide essential functions, RTO of 0-24 hours, lines of succession, alternate operating locations, IT systems, and a Critical Interruption Worksheet. HSI community college in the Rio Grande Valley.

published_revised: Linked from Department Plans page; AHEOP updated Feb 2025
evidence_quote: To be better prepared, all NMSU departments and units may use this form to complete a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) - to describe how your department will operate during an emergency and recover afterwards to be fully operational.
catalog_notes: NMSU All Hazards Continuity of Operations Plan template published by Environmental Health, Safety & Risk Management for all units. Captures department essential functions, leadership succession (head plus three successors), internal/external dependencies, mitigation strategies, exercising, and recovery. Required of all major units per the Department Plans page, alongside the All Hazards Emergency Operations Plan.

evidence_quote: To be better prepared, all UA departments, units, and colleges are required to use this form to complete a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) to describe how your area would operate during a wide range of emergencies or influenza pandemic and recover afterward to be fully operational.
catalog_notes: University of Arizona Emergency Management (Critical Incident Response Team) COOP program. Departments/units/colleges complete a COOP via a Qualtrics-based form and can download a PDF; a sample COOP is published via Box. Operating units develop Business Continuity Plans identifying impacts of losses, recovery strategies, and Emergency Operations Personnel.

evidence_quote: This policy sets forth a structure for the management of these events and the continuity of operations should an incident involve a significant disruption to the University.
catalog_notes: SMU University Policy 11.3 (Resilience and Crisis Management) establishes the continuity-of-operations framework managed by the Office of Risk Management and the Emergency Management Committee. Defines COOP as a plan with critical business impact analysis and workaround procedures; ORM maintains all department COOPs in a risk management information system (RMIS). Methodist-affiliated private university.

evidence_quote: This document is intended to provide guidance and assistance for all NAU Colleges and Departments in the development, implementation and maintenance of a business continuity program.
catalog_notes: NAU Comptroller's Office maintains the university Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan. Detailed Business Impact Analysis methodology with Maximum Acceptable Outage (MAO) recovery targets and classification of core processes into Critical, Essential, and Administrative functions. Publishes a BCDR glossary, disaster recovery steps, a critical-functions assessment template, and links to the Arizona DOA business continuity standard.

institution_name: University of Oklahoma Health Sciences Center
evidence_quote: At OUHSC, we will utilize our Continuity of Operations Plan template and quick guide, located below. We can help you identify what the mission-critical and essential functions are; determine your succession line and identify the technology needs for your team.
catalog_notes: OU Health Sciences Center Risk Management business continuity program. Provides guidance, training, planning, testing/assessment, tabletop and full-scale exercises, and a Continuity of Operations Plan template and quick guide for departments/colleges. Helps units identify mission-critical/essential functions, succession lines, and technology needs.

evidence_quote: Business continuity planning is a crucial process that identifies and prioritizes essential business operations and services that need to be maintained during disruptions on campus.
catalog_notes: UT Arlington Office of Emergency Management business continuity program. Uses the 'UT Arlington Ready Tool' (Business Impact Analysis-based online tool) to store and create department plans; publishes an interview handbook. States vision/mission of organizational resilience focused on essential functions, research activities, and academic instruction.

framework_alignment: NIMS; Texas SORM Continuity Planning; Texas Education Code Ch. 51 Sec. 217
evidence_quote: The purpose of this policy is to describe provisions to minimize the adverse effects of an emergency through the development of Emergency Management programs and Continuity of Operations Plans at the University of Houston System universities, as well as to ensure compliance with applicable statutes and policies.
catalog_notes: UH System Administrative Memorandum 01.F.04 requiring each UHS university to designate a Continuity Coordinator and develop/maintain a COOP and COOP training/exercise program per the Texas State Office of Risk Management. System office develops an enterprise Continuity of Operations Planning Program with standards and tools. Defines COOP, NIMS, and emergency management. PDF version also available.

framework_alignment: NIST SP 800-53 Contingency Planning (CP) controls; Texas Admin Code Ch. 202
evidence_quote: Institution-wide emergency plans are maintained by TWU Risk Management, per URP 04.410: Emergency Management and Business Continuity. Risk Management maintains the Texas Woman's University Continuity of Operations Plan ("COOP").
catalog_notes: TWU University Regulation and Procedure 04.775 (Information System Contingency Planning) implementing NIST SP 800-53 Contingency Planning controls (CP-1 through CP-11). Covers alternate storage/processing sites, telecommunications, system backup, recovery and reconstitution with RTO/RPO defined in the TWU COOP. Confirms Risk Management maintains the university-wide COOP under separate regulation URP 04.410. Public-facing PowerDMS policy host.

framework_alignment: FEMA continuity 10 elements; Federal and State (Texas SORM) continuity initiatives
evidence_quote: 10 Elements 1. Essential Functions (EFs) 2. Orders of Succession 3. Delegations of Authority 4. Continuity Facilities 5. Essential Records 6. Continuity Communications 7. Human Resources and Key Personnel 8. Devolution of control 9. Reconstitution 10. Tests, Training and Exercises (TT&E) Program
catalog_notes: Texas Tech University System Office of Risk Management COOP Program Overview (15-page PDF). Built on the FEMA 10 continuity elements (essential functions, orders of succession, delegations of authority, continuity facilities, essential records, continuity communications, HR/key personnel, devolution, reconstitution, TT&E). Defines 12-hour operational capability, 30-day sustainment (60 days for pandemic), and a 7-phase COOP program model with Business Process Analysis and Risk Assessment.

evidence_quote: ASU's COOP ensures critical university functions continue during disruptions or unexpected events. Each unit assigns a Continuity Coordinator to maintain its plan with Emergency Management.
catalog_notes: Arizona State University Continuity of Operations Program managed through the CFO/University Business Services Office of Risk and Emergency Management. Tier-based model: Tier I units (vital to core operations) must maintain a standalone plan; Tier II units (can pause up to 72 hours) are managed by Tier I divisions. Each unit assigns a Continuity Coordinator; plans stored in an internal COOP SharePoint, reviewed annually and updated biennially.

evidence_quote: Business Continuity is the advance planning and preparation undertaken to ensure that an organization will have the capability to operate its critical business functions during emergency events. The aim is to reduce harmful effects of potential hazards.
catalog_notes: Tarrant County College District Emergency Management & Business Continuity function within the Emergency Operations Center, led by a Director and Manager of Emergency Management (both Certified Emergency Managers). Program supports districtwide preparedness, response, recovery, and continuity of critical business functions. Community college district serving Tarrant County.

framework_alignment: Texas DIR Security Controls Catalog (NIST-based); TAMUS Regulations 34.07.01 and 34.07.02
evidence_quote: Contingency Planning Policy and associated controls describe the requirements for written plans to minimize the effects of a disaster and either maintain or quickly resume mission-critical information technology functions.
catalog_notes: Tarleton State University Security Controls Catalog, Contingency Planning (CP) family, CP-1 policy. IT contingency/continuity controls that must align with the Tarleton Emergency Operations Plan and the Tarleton Business Continuity Plan (a component of the TAMU System Offices BCP) required by TAMUS Regulation 34.07.02. Regional public university in the Texas A&M University System.

framework_alignment: Texas DIR Security Control Standards Catalog; Texas Admin Code 202; TAMUS Policy 29.01
evidence_quote: Contingency Planning (CP) Defines strategies for maintaining and restoring operations during and after a disruption, including data backup, recovery, and continuity plans.
catalog_notes: TAMU-Kingsville Information Security Controls Catalog (ITS Standard Administrative Procedures), structured by DIR control families including a Contingency Planning (CP) domain covering data backup, recovery, and continuity plans. Catalog explicitly states it supports institutional continuity and resilience. Aligned with Texas DIR Security Control Standards Catalog, TAC 202, and TAMUS Policy 29.01. HSI in the Texas A&M University System.
